commit.message:
perf(maglev): true-inline array store + IC-preserving fast path

Zero-call inline fast path for keyed stores of Smi and Boolean values:
on IC hit (same array, in bounds), write discriminant + payload bytes
directly to the element memory without any Rust function call.

Critically, in-bounds stores no longer invalidate the array IC slot.
Previously every StaKeyedProperty zeroed the IC handle, forcing the
next LdaKeyedProperty through the slow path.  Now only out-of-bounds
stores (which may cause Vec reallocation) invalidate the cache.

Added mov_store_dword_base_disp32 and mov_store_byte_imm_base_disp32
instructions to the x86-64 macro assembler for field-level writes.

Expected impact: sieve_primes benchmark (tight load/store loop on
boolean arrays) should improve dramatically since load IC now stays
valid across iterations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(adapter): remove $order field, derive child order from Object.keys()

The $order metadata field caused errors because denicek.set() could not
navigate to $order on deeply nested nodes. Instead of maintaining an
explicit order string, child ordering is now derived from the record's
field enumeration order — JavaScript objects maintain insertion order for
string keys, and the CRDT adds fields in creation order.

- Remove $order from METADATA_FIELDS, createRootNode, buildPlainRecord
- Remove readOrder, insertIntoOrder, removeFromOrder helpers
- Simplify addChildren, deleteNodes, moveNodes (no order management)
- Rewrite indexElementNode to discover children via Object.keys() scan
- Bump version to 0.2.2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

store: fix migration crash when ticket_id is already TEXT but key column remains

migrateTicketIDToText was guarded only by whether the 'key' column existed.
If the migration had partially run (ticket_id already TEXT but key column
not yet dropped), the next startup re-entered the migration and tried to
scan TEXT ticket ids (e.g. 'PXL-E-422') into int64, crashing with:

  sql: Scan error on column index 0, name "ticket_id":
  converting driver.Value type string ("PXL-E-422") to a int64

Fix: add a second guard in migrateTicketIDToText — if ticket_id is already
TEXT, skip the data migration and just drop the leftover key column.

Also adds columnType() helper alongside columnExists().

feat(copyTeamPermissions): remove allAssets DEV-1333 DEV-1334 (#6893)

### 📣 Summary

Make Copy Team Permissions feature properly load all available projects
and allow for searching particular one within the project selection
dropdown. Also stop using deprecated `allAssets` store.

### 💭 Notes

Changes here:
- Changed `CopyTeamPermissions` to functional component
- Use infinite scroll in project selection dropdown to load as many
projects as user needs (through `useInfiniteScroll` and
`InfiniteScrollTrigger`)
- `SharingForm` passes on whole locally available asset object down to
`CopyTeamPermissions` and doesn't rely on `allAssets` anymore

### 👀 Preview steps

1. ℹ️ Have an account, multiple different users, and a three projects A,
B, and C
2. Never open project C
3. Open Project A → Settings → Sharing
4. Share project with some users
5. Open Project B → Settings → Sharing
6. Notice the "Who has access" section displays single user (project
owner)
7. Toggle open "Copy team from another project"
8. 🔴 [on main] Notice that "Select source project…" dropdown only lists
project A
9. 🟢 [on PR] Notice that "Select source project…" dropdown lists all
your projects
10. Select project A
11. Click "copy" and then confirm with "PROCEED"
12. 🟢 Notice a success notification "permissions were copied
successfully"
13. 🟢 Notice the "Who has access" section now displays all the users
from point (4)

commit.message:
Fix review issues: handle leak, partial send, error loop, configurable inline

Fixes from production code review:

Critical:
- Fix MemoryHandle leak on connection abort: DisposeAsync now checks
  _sendHandle.Pointer != null, not just HasSendInFlight. Covers abort
  between Pin() and SQE submission.
- Send loop wrapped in try/catch to dispose handle on any failure.

High:
- Fix partial send data loss: send loop now retries short writes in a
  loop until full segment is transmitted.
- Add UnsafeInlineScheduling option (default true). When false, Kestrel
  HTTP processing runs on ThreadPool instead of IO thread (safer for
  blocking middleware).

Medium:
- Fix infinite error loop: after 5 consecutive errors, force-advance
  CQ head to skip corrupted CQE.

Low:
- Remove dead code: PendingSend struct, OnSendComplete method.

Tests: 4 new unit tests for correctness bugs (11/14 pass, 3 are Linux-only).
WSL2 benchmark: +8.2% vs sockets, no regression from fixes.
